name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 hot-band consumer ordering

summary.name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 hot-band consumer ordering
summary.hypothesis: The grouped_rows=16 branch was measured negative; returning to the accepted grouped_rows=8 window should recover the better PTX hot-band consumer behavior from round 56 and leave room for a narrower consumer-side tweak such as row-pair traversal order or another local ordering refinement.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Consumer-side PTX hot-band ordering and export latency in bf16_gemm_v1_tensor_core_fixed_hot_band_128x128_ptx_microkernel, especially around the grouped-row dispatch and the consumer/export handoff.
summary.risk: Moderate: a local ordering tweak can easily drift back into the grouped_rows=16 slow path or disturb the accepted PTX export-scratch behavior.
metrics_to_recheck: kernel runtime vs 24.713584 ms accepted base
summary.stop_condition: Stop if grouped_rows=8 restores the accepted runtime window and any local consumer-order tweak does not worsen dram or scoreboard metrics.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
